quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 9 Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: 9.10: Expected Value and Its Applications
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Calculating the average outcome of a random process

questions.question: A game involves rolling a fair six-sided die. You win $\$12$ if you roll a 6, and you win $\$0$ for any other number. What is the expected value of a single roll?

An insurance company sells a policy for $\$500$. If the policyholder has a claim, the company pays $\$10{,}000$. If the probability of a claim is 2%, what is the expected profit for the company per policy?
questions.explanation: The company gets $\$500$ regardless. There is a 2% chance they lose $\$10{,}000$. $E(X) = 500 - (0.02 \times 10{,}000) = 500 - 200 = \$300$ profit.

questions.question: A spinner has three sections: Red (50%), Blue (30%), and Green (20%). If Red pays $\$2$, Blue pays $\$5$, and Green pays $\$10$, what is the expected payout?
In a game, you have a 1/10 chance of winning $\$100$ and a 9/10 chance of losing $\$5$. What is the expected value of playing this game once?
A raffle sells 1,000 tickets at $\$5$ each. There is one grand prize worth $\$1{,}000$. What is the expected value of one ticket (considering the cost to play)?
questions.explanation: Value = $(1/1000 \times 1000) - 5 = 1 - 5 = -\$4.00$. On average, a player loses $\$4$ per ticket.

questions.question: A basketball player is in a '1-and-1' free throw situation. If he makes the first (60% chance), he gets a second shot. If he misses the first, he gets 0 points. Each shot is worth 1 point. What is his expected number of points?
A game costs $\$3$ to play. You win $\$10$ if a coin lands heads, and $\$0$ if tails. Is this a fair game? What is the expected profit?
questions.explanation: Expected winnings = $10(0.5) + 0(0.5) = \$5$. Subtract $\$3$ cost: profit $= \$2$. A fair game has $E = 0$. Player has the advantage.

questions.question: An investor is looking at a stock. There is a 40% chance it gains $\$5{,}000$ and a 60% chance it loses $\$2{,}000$. What is the expected gain/loss?

A test has a penalty for guessing. You get 1 point for a correct answer and $-0.25$ points for an incorrect answer. If a question has 5 options and you guess randomly, what is the expected value for that question?
A warranty for a laptop costs $\$100$. There is a 5% chance the laptop will need a $\$1{,}500$ repair. What is the expected value of the warranty for the customer?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a great grasp of mathematical expectation — calculating expected value, profit vs. cost, fair games, insurance and warranty applications, and multi-stage probability.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Key reminders: (1) $E(X) = \sum (\text{outcome} \times \text{probability})$, (2) Subtract the cost to play when calculating net profit, (3) A fair game has expected profit $= 0$, (4) For multi-stage events, list all outcomes and their probabilities.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Identifying all possible outcomes and their probabilities, (2) Including negative outcomes (losses) in the calculation, (3) Subtracting the cost to play or purchase from expected winnings, (4) Expected value is a long-run average — not a guarantee for any single trial.
scoreMessages.0: Review expected value: (1) $E(X) = \sum (x_i \cdot p_i)$ — multiply each outcome by its probability and add, (2) For profit, subtract the cost from expected winnings, (3) A fair game has $E = 0$, positive means player advantage, negative means house advantage, (4) List all possible outcomes first, then compute.
